question.title: Employee Salary Ranking
question.desc: Rank employees based on their salary within their respective departments. The ranking should be based on the highest salary first. If two employees have the same salary, they should have the same rank.
question.setup: DROP TABLE IF EXISTS departments; CREATE TABLE departments (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, name VARCHAR(255)); INSERT INTO departments (name) VALUES ('Sales'), ('Marketing'), ('IT'); DROP TABLE IF EXISTS employees; CREATE TABLE employees (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, name VARCHAR(255), department_id INTEGER, salary DECIMAL(10, 2), FOREIGN KEY (department_id) REFERENCES departments (id)); INSERT INTO employees (name, department_id, salary) VALUES ('John Doe', 1, 50000.00), ('Jane Doe', 1, 60000.00), ('Bob Smith', 2, 40000.00), ('Alice Johnson', 2, 55000.00), ('Mike Brown', 3, 70000.00), ('Emily Davis', 3, 65000.00), ('Tom White', 1, 45000.00), ('Linda Lee', 2, 35000.00), ('David Kim', 3, 58000.00);
question.solution: SELECT e.name, d.name AS department_name, e.salary, DENSE_RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY d.id ORDER BY e.salary DESC) AS rank FROM employees e JOIN departments d ON e.department_id = d.id ORDER BY d.id, e.salary DESC;
question.tips: Consider using DENSE_RANK() instead of RANK() to avoid gaps in the ranking.
hints: You need to join the employees table with the departments table to get the department name.
Use the DENSE_RANK() window function to rank employees by salary within each department.
Make sure to partition the data by department_id and order by salary in descending order.

tests.setup: DROP TABLE IF EXISTS departments; CREATE TABLE departments (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, name VARCHAR(255)); INSERT INTO departments (name) VALUES ('Finance'), ('HR'), ('Operations'); DROP TABLE IF EXISTS employees; CREATE TABLE employees (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, name VARCHAR(255), department_id INTEGER, salary DECIMAL(10, 2), FOREIGN KEY (department_id) REFERENCES departments (id)); INSERT INTO employees (name, department_id, salary) VALUES ('Sarah Taylor', 1, 62000.00), ('Kevin Martin', 1, 52000.00), ('Rebecca Hall', 2, 48000.00), ('James Wilson', 2, 38000.00), ('Olivia Thompson', 3, 68000.00), ('William Lewis', 3, 58000.00);

DROP TABLE IF EXISTS departments; CREATE TABLE departments (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, name VARCHAR(255)); INSERT INTO departments (name) VALUES ('Research'), ('Development'), ('Quality Assurance'); DROP TABLE IF EXISTS employees; CREATE TABLE employees (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, name VARCHAR(255), department_id INTEGER, salary DECIMAL(10, 2), FOREIGN KEY (department_id) REFERENCES departments (id)); INSERT INTO employees (name, department_id, salary) VALUES ('Peter Davis', 1, 70000.00), ('Lisa Nguyen', 1, 60000.00), ('Daniel Lee', 2, 55000.00), ('Hannah Kim', 2, 50000.00), ('Michael Patel', 3, 65000.00), ('Sofia Rodriguez', 3, 58000.00);
